title: kube: plug HostUsers in the pod spec
body:
map HostUsers=false to userns=auto.

One difference with the current implementation in the Kubelet is that the podman default size is 1024 while the Kubelet uses 65536.

This is done on purpose, because 65536 is a problem for rootless as the entire IDs space would be allocated to a single pod.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

Should we go the other way as well
podman generate kube?

> Should we go the other way as well
> podman generate kube?

added another patch to plug it into `podman generate kube`

How is this supposed to be used?  I run rootless podman and I do not pass `--userns` to `podman run`.  Nevertheless the userns is utilized (by /etc/subgid and /etc/subuid: I have different users within the container, or withing `unshare`), so I assume there is implicit `--userns` for the current user.

In any case at https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html#userns-mode `--userns=auto` and `--userns=nomap` seem to do the same thing (according to the table), 

The above link says for `--userns`: 
> It defaults to the PODMAN_USERNS environment variable

I have no PODMAN_USERNS environment variable.  To what does it default now?

Finally, I run a rootless container with a volume.hostpath.Type=directory.  It does run under rootless pod.  It used to work under CRI-O when the container has the Annotation:io.kubernetes.cri-o.userns-mode: "auto".

Currently it does not work on my system, since crio stopped mounting containers — https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/issues/6189 .

What I am saying is, that with the `io.kubernetes.cri-o.userns-mode: "auto"` annotation and CRI-O I was able to use volume.hostpath.Type=directory. .  hostUsers: false does not allows this volume form — https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/user-namespaces/#limitations .  So the kube generate export might not work here.

There is no way to use rootless Podman without a user namespace. We use an overall user namespace (we call this the rootless user namespace) in which all rootless Podman processes for a given user run, which gives us access to more than one UID and GID, the ability to mount tmpfs, fuse, and (on newer kernels) overlay filesystems. Containers can create their own user namespace under this overall user namespace (useful to allow reordering UIDs and GIDs to place the UID of the user running Podman in a specific place; see `--user=keep-id`). The container's user namespace is controlled by `--userns`. The overall rootless user namespace cannot be controlled; it is mandatory, as we cannot perform critical operations without it. You can, however, force its recreation with `podman system migrate` (as you may need to do if `/etc/subuid` is modified).
In rootless podman I type `podman kube generate …`.  Why doesn’t the output contain `spec.hostUsers: false`?

inside the rootless user namespace, pods/containers can have their own as a child.  `spec.hostUsers` would be true if a pod/container is using  separate user namespace.  For the rootless user, the initial namespace created is intended as `host`.
